channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Build an AI dubbing pipeline: faster-whisper + XTTS-v2 + FFmpeg
item.description: TL;DR We're building a script that takes a video in English and produces the same video narrated in Spanish, in a cloned version of the original speaker's voice. Stack: faster-whisper for...

item.title: Probing FFmpeg's av1_vulkan encoder: does your GPU actually support it?
item.description: TL;DR FFmpeg 8.x includes av1_vulkan, the first cross-vendor GPU AV1 encoder in mainline FFmpeg. We'll probe whether your GPU + driver actually expose AV1 encode, run a first working encode,...

item.title: Ship a 'Go Live' button: OBS in, LL-HLS out, webhooks in between
item.description: TL;DR We're adding live streaming to a SaaS dashboard: a backend endpoint that creates a stream, OBS as the broadcaster over RTMPS, LL-HLS playback with hls.js, and a webhook handler that keeps...

item.title: How to Prevent Duplicate API Processing with Idempotency in MuleSoft
item.description: Duplicate processing is one of those integration problems that looks small in development but becomes painful in production. A client retries an API call because the response timed out. A queue...

item.title: Never Deploy Django Without These 6 Things
item.description: Deploying a Django app feels great, until something breaks in production. Everything may work locally, but once you deploy, you might run into missing styles, database errors, or an app that won’t...

item.title: The Economics of Local LLMs: Why Practical Models Win in African Tech
item.description: One thing I deeply appreciate about Gemma is that Google appears to understand a reality many AI companies conveniently ignore: most developers are not sitting on clusters of H100s. Most of us are...

item.title: 15-Year-Old GhostLock Flaw Enables Root and Container Escape on Most Linux Distros
item.description: Researchers at Nebula Security have disclosed GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499), a 15-year-old Linux kernel flaw that lets any logged-in user take full root control of a machine that has not been...

item.title: Bootstrapped Data Products — Here's What I Learned
item.description: I'm Heshan. I build data products. No VC, no team — just me, a lot of Playwright scripts, and PostgreSQL. Two years ago I couldn't build anything that worked. Today I run three production data...

item.title: What should a modern Go dependency injection framework look like?
item.description: Go DI has always been a trade-off. Runtime DI frameworks provide a convenient API, but they rely on reflection and discover dependency problems during application startup. Compile-time DI...

item.title: Digital Logic Design : Nobody teaches you this!
item.description: So I am an Electrical Engineering Student!I was looking for the blogs or GitHub repo's for months to help me understand some beginner level concepts in DLD but not able to find any one of that so...

item.title: Every Developer Works with JSON. Here Are the Tools I Use the Most.
item.description: If you’re building APIs, web apps, or mobile apps, you’re probably staring at JSON every single day. Some days you’re just trying to validate a massive API response. Other days, you’re playing...

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Actively Exploited Adobe, Joomla, and Langflow Flaws to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added four security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation....

item.title: Meta’s glasses will turn off the camera if you tamper with the privacy light
item.description: Amid public backlash over its smart glasses, Meta announced that it will be updating its glasses with a new feature that will disable the camera when it detects that someone has tampered with or...

item.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
item.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

item.title: Meta Now Lets Anyone Use Your Instagram Photos in AI Images—Unless You Opt Out
item.description: As part of Meta’s Muse Image model rollout, Instagram users with public accounts need to opt out to block AI generations of their content.

item.title: Of course viewers are giving up on Netflix shows
item.description: Even though Netflix is the world's most popular paid streaming service, the company has been struggling to keep viewers watching its series after their first seasons. Beef - the streamer's...

item.title: Netflix is about to host videos from BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, and other publishers
item.description: Starting on August 3rd, Netflix's streaming library will include video content from dozens of digital media brands including BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, Hearst Magazines, People Inc, and Tastemade. As...

item.title: OpenAI’s Chief Futurist Is Leaving the Company
item.description: Joshua Achiam spent nearly nine years at OpenAI researching AI safety and made a memorable appearance in the Musk v. Altman trial.

item.title: From Hugging Face to Amazon SageMaker Studio in one click
Meta’s new Muse Image model can pull other Instagram users into AI photos
item.description: Meta is launching the first AI image generation model made by its Superintelligence Labs division. The Muse Image model now powers the image-making tools across the Meta AI app, Instagram, and...

item.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
item.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

item.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
item.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

item.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
item.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

item.title: X says top accounts steal videos from other users as it announces new video tools
item.description: Nikita Bier, X's head of product, said in a post on Monday that "[m]any videos from top accounts are simply stolen from other users, sometimes 5 years after they originally went viral," while...

item.title: The ‘G-Wagen of golf carts’ could be the ideal second car
item.description: While the auto industry wrings its hands over the electric vehicle market, sweating details like aerodynamic efficiency and range anxiety, a new EV startup based in Lisbon, Portugal, is zagging in...

item.title: ABC tells the government to get out of its newsrooms
item.description: ABC is firing back at the Federal Communications Commission after the agency opened an investigation into The View's airtime of political candidates. In a letter to the FCC on Tuesday, ABC argues...

item.title: Anthropic is launching Claude Cowork on mobile and web
item.description: Starting Tuesday, Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI platform will be available on mobile and web for the first time. The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude...

item.title: Google announces Pixel 11 launch event in August
item.description: Google is hosting its next Made by Google launch event for Pixel hardware on August 12th in New York City, according to an invitation sent by Google to The Verge. Unusually, the event is taking...

item.title: RedWing MaaS Packages Android Bank Fraud as a Telegram Rental Service
item.description: A new Android malware operation called RedWing is being rented out on Telegram as a ready-made bank-fraud service. It lets even low-skill criminals take over a victim's phone, steal their banking...

item.title: Rogue Agent Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Hijack Google Dialogflow CX Chatbots
item.description: A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project....
